sections.biography-source-undefined:
Cao Chi, styled Yuansheng, was from Qiao county, Pei commandery. He was the father of Cao Ren and Cao Chun, and the older brother of Cao Yin [曹胤]. He served as Palace Grandee, Chief Clerk under a Major, Palace Attendant, and Commandant of the Chang River Encampments under the Han. When he was thirty-eight, he died. He would be posthumously made Marquis of Chen by Cao Pi to commend the achievements Cao Ren rendered to the nation.

In modern times, the tombs of the Cao clan were excavated, and tomb bricks indicated that Cao Chi was styled Yuansheng, something not mentioned in extant records.

full_text:
Cao Chi (Yuansheng) 曹熾 (元盛)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Cao Chi (曹熾), styledYuansheng (元盛), was born in Qiao county, Pei commandery. Father of Cao Ren and Cao Chun, held Han government titles. Cao Chi was affiliated with the Han Dynasty. Cao Chi lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Cao Chi, styled Yuansheng, was from Qiao county, Pei commandery. He was the father of Cao Ren and Cao Chun, and the older brother of Cao Yin [曹胤]. He served as Palace Grandee, Chief Clerk under a Major, Palace Attendant, and Commandant of the Chang River Encampments under the Han. When he was thirty-eight, he died. He would be posthumously made Marquis of Chen by Cao Pi to commend the achievements Cao Ren rendered to the nation.
In modern times, the tombs of the Cao clan were excavated, and tomb bricks indicated that Cao Chi was styled Yuansheng, something not mentioned in extant records.
